meaning: adj.(指人、身体)柔软的,易弯的
examples: His walk was lithe and graceful.
Just an impression of graceful, lithe forms. Then nothing.
Seamlessly, the dancers move from breakdance to something more lithe.
Tall, lithe and muscled, he is only 19 but is confident to the point of cockiness.
The ride has a sense of authority that made it lithe but solid in wintery conditions.
